title: ADP assay (Colorimetric)
author: Kiichi Hirota

steps_raw.instruction: Reconstitute the ADP standard (1 µmol) in 100 µl of water to generate a 10 mM ADP stock solution.
Dilute 10 µl of the 10 mM ADP stock solution with 90 µl of ADP Assay Buffer to prepare a 1 mM ADP standard solution.
Prepare dilutions of a ADP standard using ADP Assay Buffer (0, 0.04, 0.08, 0.12, 0.16, 0.2 mM). 100 µl of each dilution is required to set up duplicate reading (2 x 50 µl).
Remove media and wash cells with cold PBS.
Lyse cells in 100 µl of ADP Assay Buffer.
Incubate the lysate for 10 min on ice.
Centrifuge at 18,000 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C.
Collect the supernatant in a new microcentrifuge tube and keep on ice.
Prepare 50 µl of Reaction Mix for each reaction. Prepare an amount of Reaction Mix sufficient for all samples including ADP standards. %s %s
Add 50 µl of ADP standards or samples per well to a clear 96-well plate.
Add 50 µl of Reaction Mix to each well.
Incubate for 30 min at room temperature.
Measure the absorbance at 750 nm using iMark™ Microplate Absorbance Reader.
Test all samples with a minimum of two replicates.
Dilute the lysate 3-fold in ADP Assay Buffer.
